user_prompt: I am testing a security level 1 build, I have activated the default secure soft Wi-Fi AP, I have loaded the front-end web page. I attempted to login to it, the error message I got is `n is not a constructor`
if something is seriously wrong with the webcrypto shim and requires it to be rebuilt, let me know
agent_reply: Yes, the WebCrypto shim was genuinely broken for this AP use case.

The old `webcrypto-shim.min.js` expected runtime globals like `self.asmCrypto`, but the page only loaded the shim itself. On an HTTP soft AP where native `crypto.subtle` is unavailable, HMAC/AES-GCM then fell into missing shim internals, producing `n is not a constructor`.

I rebuilt [html/webcrypto-shim.min.js](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/html/webcrypto-shim.min.js>) with the crypto implementation bundled in, then regenerated [inc/web_assets.h](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/web_assets.h>) so firmware embeds the fixed page.
- local no-native-`subtle` smoke test: HMAC works, AES-GCM encrypt/decrypt round-trips
I left the pre-existing edits in `lib/Security/web-access-security-policy.md`, `src/main.cpp`, and `src/version.c` alone.
